title: Creating Items using the new items_page
excerpt:
Hey there, hope all is well! Am a little new to Monday Dev and GraphQL! 
I’ve looked at the new docs regarding the new items_page api. However, I did not see anything about Creating or Mutating an Item using this new API…

posts.author: paul1204 (Paul)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey there, hope all is well! Am a little new to Monday Dev and GraphQL!

I’ve looked at the new docs regarding the new items_page api. However, I did not see anything about Creating or Mutating an Item using this new API. Am I missing something? How do we go about creating/mutating an item using the items_page?

I did see under Items “If you’re getting items… use items_page”, I’m guessing items_page is only for retrieving info since it says “getting”?

Here’s a link of docs I’ve read over

monday API docs

Items

❗️The behaviour of this query will change in version 2023-10If you're getting items from a specific board or group, use use items_page instead of items. You can still query items at the root to retrieve specific items by their ID.Items are core...

monday API docs

Items page

Every board has advanced filters that enable you to retrieve items based on specified criteria quickly. You can replicate this behavior via the API using the items_page object and customized parameters to filter your results. As the monday.com...

monday API docs

Items page by column values

👍Only available in API version 2023-10 and laterThe items_page_by_column_values object is only available in API version 2023-10 and later. It replaces the deprecated items_by_column_values and items_by_multiple_column_values objects.Items are core...

Cheers!

posts.author: joseph.monday (Joseph Laux)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Paul,

Welcome to the community! Hope you are enjoying it here!

As you mentioned, items_page is used to retrieve items. The behavior to create items is not changing. Within the Items documentation, you will use create_item, as linked to the section here.

Hope this helps and happy developing!

Best,
Joseph

posts.author: paul1204 (Paul)
posts.cookedHtml:
Joseph Laux:

ithin the Items documentation, you will use create_item, as linked t

Great! Thank you for your response.

Best,
Paul

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello there @paul1204,

I am glad @joseph.monday’s answer was what you needed

Let us know if you need anything else!

Cheers,
Matias
